free_text: Production cross sections for Be-7 and Na-22 in targets
of Si,Mg,Fe and Ni irradiated by 1,2,3 and 23 GeV
protons.
The results are compared with a recent-
semiempirical formula.
80 cm3 Ge(Li) detector having 3.5 keV
resolution at 1.3 MeV
Target stacks at 1,2 and 3 GeV are rectangles of
20*6 mm and at 23 GeV are disks of 25 mm diam.
.For 1,2,3 GeV protons.
.For 23 GeV protons.
The count rate corrected to time zero
Total uncertainty including:
Uncertainty in decomposing the peaks,as
well as the statistical error (usually less 2%).
Uncertainty in counting rate
Uncertainty in number of target atoms
2.the number of target atoms,included the possible
effects of misalignment and non-uniformity as well as
weighting errors approximately 1%.
The uncertainty in the half-life.
The uncertainty in the counter efficiency.
The uncertainty in the branching ratios.
Tbl.2 from PR/C,12,915,1975
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Major corrections in Subents 002-017.
The integrated proton flux was 5 - 10. x 10**15.
.For 1,2,3 GeV protons.
Silicon stack foils,50 mg/cm**2 thickness,were used.
/By authors/ The value given by Cumming is 10.8 mb,
while that of Tobailem et al. is 11.1 mb. On the other
hand Radin et al. reported a value at 740 MeV that is
-10% lower than given by those authors. As a compromise
we have adopted 10.4 mb and assign a 10% uncertainty
to this value.
.The uncertainty in the monitor reaction.
SD: RAD-DET deleted (same in DECAY-DATA);

number was corrected (CEA-N-1446 -> CEA-N-1466(1)).
The integrated proton flux was 5 - 10. x 10**15.
.For 1,2,3 GeV protons.
Silicon stack foils,50 mg/cm**2 thickness,were used.
.The uncertainty in the monitor reaction.
